frontier: Erdős #979: smallest N = sum of three prime cubes in exactly 6 ways (OEIS A385316 a(6)); current best lower bound a(6) > 499,243,435,237.
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Copied the accepted-continuation verifier artifact (res_20260723_145749/verifier_prime_cubes.py, identical logic) into the current session directory and ran its isolated no-network replay. Confirmed it exits 0 with all domain and predicate kill fixtures rejecting correctly with proper branch reasons. Verified all five OEIS terms a(1)..a(5) reproduce. The C=10^13 bounded exact search from the next gate cannot proceed because DGX SSH is unreachable (dgx: DNS resolution failure; spark-38e3.local: SSH key rejected).
verified:
Verifier domain: N must be a non-negative integer; the input specifies a cutoff C (maximum value to search) and k (the target multiplicity). Any non-integer, negative, or zero input is rejected.
Verifier predicate: An unordered triple (p, q, r) of primes with p <= q <= r is counted if p^3 + q^3 + r^3 <= C; the number of such triples summing to exactly N must equal k. The verifier exhaustively enumerates the complete prime set P = {p prime : p^3 <= C} and all nondecreasing triples, counts exact multiplicities per sum, and checks whether sum_counts[N] == k.
Artifact: verifier_prime_cubes.py, SHA-256: 7fe349ef1a4a829ed3f7c0c99da0ae2f9735670b21b0dea925e4b079bf1b11c7
Domain kill fixtures: 4/4 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 4/4 rejected correctly
result: Verifier artifact is clean and replayable (exit 0, all kill fixtures pass, all five known terms reproduce). No new mathematical evidence: the C=10^13 bounded exact search remains blocked by DGX infrastructure failure. Prior quarantined verifier (sha256:93cae1...) failed because of a missing CLI argument and an overly-small cutoff for a(5); the repaired version resolves both.
next_gate: When DGX SSH is restored (DNS resolution for dgx or authorized key for spark-38e3.local), execute: `ssh dgx 'python3 ~/cultural-soliton-observatory/research_sessions/res_20260723_145749_erdos-979-prime-cubes-a385316/verifier_prime_cubes.py --cutoff 10000000000000'` and report full output. If zero k=6 candidates found, certify a(6) > 10^13 and advance to C=10^14.
